title: In-sensor neuromorphic computing
desc: To overcome the severe latency and power constraints of traditional machine vision, our lab pioneers in-sensor neuromorphic computing by directly integrating sensing and computation at the pixel level. Instead of shuttling massive amounts of raw data to external processors, we embed analog computing circuits, event-driven architectures, and engineered optical elements-such as designed metasurfaces-right where photons are captured. This pixel-level convergence enables native, real-time visual processing directly within the sensor array, slashing data movement and energy consumption by orders of magnitude to build the ultra-low-power, highly responsive edge vision systems essential for next-generation autonomous robotics, edge AI, and smart IoT.

title: Metasurface optical encoder
desc: Our research develops a highly versatile metasurface optical encoder engineered for computational optical sensing and advanced optical computing. By utilizing a customized random metasurface, we achieve a deterministic physical encoding that instantaneously transforms complex optical fields into expressive spatial speckle patterns. This encoding mechanism drives two primary applications. First, for computational optical sensing, the encoder captures hard-to-measure physical properties of light, such as multidimensional phase or spectral information. By combining the well-defined forward model of our metasurface with advanced computational reconstruction algorithms, we enable highly accurate retrieval of hidden physical states from simple intensity measurements. Second, for optical neural networks, the metasurface acts as a passive computational kernel that physically performs high-dimensional matrix multiplications. When integrated with event-driven neuromorphic sensors, this system selectively extracts essential features to drastically reduce data bandwidth, establishing an ultra-low-power paradigm for edge AI. Ultimately, our metasurface encoder provides a unified hardware platform that advances precision computational metrology and energy-efficient machine vision.

title: Heterogeneous integration
desc: Our lab focuses on the heterogeneous integration of III-V compound semiconductors. To overcome structural mismatches with conventional platforms, we use epitaxial lift-off to detach semiconductor thin films from their original substrates. Through transfer printing and cold-weld bonding, we seamlessly integrate these materials onto target platforms without defects. This technology enables next-generation flexible optoelectronic devices by transferring these films onto polymer-based flexible substrates. A key application is the creation of bio-inspired curved image sensors. Ultimately, by overcoming traditional planar constraints, we aim to deliver innovative form factors for robotics, wearables, and healthcare.
